content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 28, 1994

Peter Bagatelos
BAGATELOS & FADEM
601 California Street
Suite 1801
San Francisco, CA 94108

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File Nos. A-93-309a

Dear Mr. Bagatelos:

This letter is in response to your request for
reconsideration of the advice provided to San Francisco County
Supervisor Angela Alioto in the above referenced letter. This
letter also shall serve to confirm our telephone conversation of
February 23, 1994, in which I informed you that under the
circumstances described in your letter, Supervisor Alioto will not
have to reimburse the Chengdu Government for the portion of the
gift that she accepted which was in excess of the $1,000 gift
limit.

We understand that the ethics statutes¹ are relatively new
and that our interpretation of the applicable statute² may have
been one of first impression. We also understand that Supervisor
Alioto may have reasonably relied upon what she believed was good
advice in accepting the gift of travel expenses from the Chengdu
Government for her entourage in connection with her recent trip to
China. Therefore, we agree that equitable considerations and
fairness dictate we revise the requirement for reimbursement in
her case.³ Nevertheless, Supervisor Alioto is still required to
report the full value of this gift.

_____________
¹ Chapter 9.5 of the Political Reform Act, §§ 89501-89521.

² Government Code § 89501.

³ The legal advice rendered in the letter stands. When a public
official takes action exercising direction or control over a
gift, the public official has received or accepted the gift.
(Government Code § 82028; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18941.)
In Supervisor Alioto's case, the gift she accepted was an all-expense
paid trip to China for herself and for
four persons designated by her to accompanying her to China. The
portion of the gift that was for the travel expenses of her
entourage was subject to the $1,000 limit.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-309a
Page 2




If you have any further questions concerning this advice,
please don't hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[signature: Deanne Stone]
By: Deanne Stone
Senior Commission Counsel

sections.conclusion: Supervisor Alioto will not have to reimburse the Chengdu Government for the portion of the gift in excess of the $1,000 gift limit under the circumstances described, though she remains required to report the full value of the gift.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether San Francisco County Supervisor Angela Alioto must reimburse the Chengdu Government for a gift of travel expenses for herself and her entourage that exceeded the $1,000 gift limit under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC revised its prior requirement for reimbursement based on equitable considerations and Supervisor Alioto's reasonable reliance on what she believed was FPPC advice, though the legal interpretation that the gift exceeded the $1,000 limit stands and reporting of the full gift value is still required.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a reconsideration letter responding to a prior advice letter. No separate QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections are formally marked, but the substance of both are clearly present in the body text. The letter confirms prior telephone conversation and revises the reimbursement requirement.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether San Francisco County Supervisor Angela Alioto must reimburse the Chengdu Government for a gift of travel expenses for herself and her entourage that exceeded the $1,000 gift limit under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Supervisor Alioto will not have to reimburse the Chengdu Government for the portion of the gift in excess of the $1,000 gift limit under the circumstances described, though she remains required to report the full value of the gift.

embedding.summary: The FPPC reconsidered its prior advice regarding a gift of travel expenses to San Francisco County Supervisor Angela Alioto from the Chengdu Government and agreed to waive the reimbursement requirement while maintaining the reporting requirement based on fairness and reasonable reliance.
